reasoning: The question resolves exactly to whether the WEI values for the respective resolution dates are higher than the WEI value corresponding to 2026-01-04. The FRED history fetched directly before the forecast cutoff reflects a newly published value for 2026-01-03 at 1.88. The 1.88 reading occurs in the week containing the New Year's holiday, which historically creates artificially low y/y readings for non-seasonally adjusted datasets like the WEI, owing to shifting day-to-day alignments of holiday downtimes against typical weekdays in the previous year. In 2025, a similar dip to 1.98 was observed for the exact equivalent post-New-Year week, followed by a swift and reliable rebound to >2.28 in the subsequent weeks. Given the general structural trend of the WEI hovering near 2.30 for much of late 2025 (e.g. 2.2-2.5 averages), a bounce-back by resolving observation week 2026-01-11 (mapping to Jan 10) is extremely likely. In accordance with the prompt requirement perfectly, uncertainty is sequentially increased with longer time horizons since compounding macro shocks theoretically have greater probability to push base readings down over timespans of 1–3 months (February and April). Therefore, the probabilities are set high initially to capture the statistical anomaly bounce, and smoothly taper towards 0.5.
